styles.positive_style_prompt: restrained documentary-surreal image, one clear subject or scene, subject remains recognizable after simplification, one dominant anomaly or contradiction, controlled palette, restrained lighting, clear silhouette, low clutter, non-spectacular tension, photographic documentary realism, restrained Nikon still-frame feel, documentary-cinematic vertical still, motion-ready depth
styles.negative_prompt: text, watermark, logo, ui overlay, readable signage, letters, numbers, black caption box, poster design, collage fragments, diagram, CAD render, white studio background, isolated product render, cutaway model, exploded view, red abstract object, red cube, red origami shape, red sculpture, saturated accent block, warning label, crowd chaos, collapse reenactment, gore, bodies, emergency spectacle, explosion, fireball, debris field, plastic CGI surfaces, flattened render look, pseudo NASA logo, NASA meatball, NASA worm, mission patch, agency insignia, American flag, flag decal, USA marking, United States marking, Challenger wordmark, shuttle name text, wing text, body decals, countdown clock, readable control panel, whiteboard writing, technical wall chart, posted paper, binder spine label, folder label, paperwork text, chart labels, diagram marks, keyboard text, monitor text, warning label, button labels, numbered display, digital countdown, schematic arrows, label plate, printed plate, gauge numbers, switch labels, readable film edge, contact sheet captions, shelf label, bin label, storage tag, barcode, handwritten mark, typed mark, screen interface, monitor data, folder, folders, tray, trays, binder, binders, paperwork, conference table, boardroom table, drab conference room, tabletop o-ring, small loose rubber ring

cases.prompt_body: Documentary-cinematic vertical low-angle view of a shuttle-era launch stack held by gantry steel in damp coastal dawn air, the stack partially veiled by pale ground fog and steel depth, operational scale rather than heroic poster framing, blue-gray restrained light, no ignition, no plume, no readable markings, no flags, no logos

Compressed vertical documentary view down shuttle launch flame trench and crawlerway geometry after routine operations, massive concrete channels and gantry shadows receding into cold haze, one calm light sweep across damp surfaces, routine infrastructure made tense by scale and repetition, no active launch, no countdown, no signage, no posters, no readable markings

Documentary-cinematic vertical close view of a full-scale solid rocket booster field-joint band curving past the camera, low angle along pale metal casing, frost rime catching blue-gray dawn light, bolt heads receding diagonally toward gantry steel in the background, one dark soot line trapped in the seam, tactile full-scale hardware, parallax-ready foreground and background depth, no labels, no diagrams, no flame, no smoke, no readable markings

Extreme documentary macro of a full-scale booster gasket boundary, pale cold metal curving out of frame, one localized dark soot smear and eroded seam under shallow inspection light, frost crystals on nearby fasteners, tactile material evidence that stays physically mounted and large, no loose ring, no tabletop, no labels, no arrows, no diagram, no flame

Photoreal vertical close detail of an inspection light grazing across a cold gasket boundary on full-scale launch hardware, the light reveals one narrow dark path along the seal while everything else remains mechanically calm, foreground metal edge and background gantry blur create depth for motion, no flame, no smoke jet, no labels, no cutaway, no readable markings

Cold exterior hardware detail of a solid rocket booster field joint under blue dawn light, frost gathering unevenly around one stressed seam and bolt row, full-scale cylindrical casing visible enough to avoid tabletop scale, the surface looks survivable and wrong at the same time, no loose rubber ring, no tray, no diagram, no labels, no flame

Telephoto vertical view of repeated pale booster casing bands receding past gantry steel, many identical seams normalized by perspective, one dark seam almost lost among the rhythm, restrained industrial blue-gray light and slight haze, the warning absorbed into pattern and scale, no labels, no warning words, no arrows, no diagrams, no readable markings

Photoreal close view of a wordless physical launch-control indicator field, rows of unmarked glass indicator lenses receding into dark green-gray depth, one dim amber lens swallowed by a larger field of calm lights, shallow focus and parallax-ready foreground, no numbers, no labels, no warning words, no screen interface, no monitor data, no status wall text

Documentary-cinematic view through launch-facility glass at night, anonymous engineer silhouette standing at a threshold, distant launch pad floodlights reflected in the glass, cold blue exterior light and a hard diagonal shadow splitting the frame, foreground railing and cable crossing create depth, the room falls away into darkness, no conference table, no paperwork, no readable signage, no logos, no modern devices

Late-1980s launch-facility window and interior threshold, distant pad lights reflected across glass, a hard diagonal shadow divides the frame like a burden shifting, one anonymous engineer silhouette small and still near the edge, cold exterior scale dominates over the room, no table, no documents, no whiteboard, no readable signs, no logos, no modern devices

Narrow institutional launch-facility corridor leading toward a cold blue pad light beyond glass, a single cable and metal railing cross the threshold in the foreground, deep perspective and hard light make the decision space physical, no people at a table, no paperwork, no wall text, no room numbers, no modern screens, no conference room

Vertical documentary close view of icy launch tower steel in blue dawn, frost and icicles on gantry structure with pale shuttle-era hardware looming behind, cold morning made visible through texture and scale, slow vapor or haze implied by layered depth, no countdown clock, no flag, no logo, no readable markings, no explosion

Restrained vertical sky and pad geometry after ignition moment without spectacle, white vapor trail slicing upward past hard gantry silhouettes and cold blue morning, consequence suggested through scale, speed, and empty vertical space rather than fireball, no explosion, no debris, no bodies, no readable shuttle markings, no logos, no countdown

Extreme foreground macro of a tiny dark seal flaw on pale cold launch hardware, the flaw sharp and small while full launch infrastructure looms out of focus behind it, focus relationship makes a minor accepted defect visually control the whole frame, tactile soot and frost, restrained blue-gray light, no text, no labels, no diagram, no explosion, no memorial imagery

Extreme foreground macro of a tiny scorched seal seam on pale cold launch hardware, the small dark line casting an oversized hard shadow across nearby booster casing and gantry steel, launch infrastructure looms out of focus behind it, disproportionate consequence expressed only through scale and shadow, restrained blue-gray dawn light, tactile metal and soot, no text, no labels, no diagram, no explosion, no memorial imagery
